indication: Transient episode of right-sided weakness and word-finding difficulty, evaluate for acute intracranial hemorrhage or mass lesion. Patient on anticoagulation for atrial fibrillation.
technique: Non-contrast helical CT of the head was performed with 5 mm axial sections from the skull base to the vertex.

findings.parenchyma: No acute intracranial hemorrhage. No acute territorial infarct identified. Chronic small vessel ischemic changes with periventricular hypodensities. Small chronic lacunar infarct in the left basal ganglia, measuring approximately 5 mm.
findings.ventricles: Lateral ventricles are mildly prominent, likely representing ex vacuo changes related to generalized volume loss. No hydrocephalus. Third and fourth ventricles are normal in size.
findings.extraAxial: No epidural or subdural hematoma. No subarachnoid hemorrhage.
findings.sinuses: Mucosal thickening in the bilateral maxillary sinuses. Remaining paranasal sinuses and mastoid air cells are clear.

impression: No acute intracranial hemorrhage or large territorial infarct.
No midline shift or mass effect.
Chronic small vessel ischemic disease with periventricular white matter hypodensities.
Small chronic lacunar infarct in the left basal ganglia.
Mild generalized cerebral volume loss consistent with age.
recommendations: If clinical concern for acute ischemic stroke persists, MRI with diffusion-weighted imaging is recommended for further evaluation
comparison: No prior CT head available for comparison.
